_nodes.description:
The Druids of Fernalla believe that a powerful artifact exists known as 'A Branch of the Great Willow' - the tree from which Fernalla observes the land. 

You should note of any clues you stumble upon, and follow their trails.
You gave a tiny statue of a talking cat to a ghostly talking deer in a dream, and the deer gave you a nightmare.
_nodes.disabled_text: No thank you I have one already!

_nodes.description: You've heard a legend that if you were to approach the Gate of Fernalla with a worthy gift, it may open for you.

An old Hermit in Fernalla's Field has asked you to recover an old toolbox from a group of bandits who are passing through the area. 

If you are able to recover it, bring it back to him at his home.
_nodes.disabled_text: What sorcery is this... that is not mine! I have mine here!

_nodes.description:
There is a group of people hidden underneath Port Azure near the docks. A spoken password is required for safe entry - it's "Shadowtide".

You should investigate when you're in the city.

A note was given to you by a strange hermit in Fernalla's Revival Plains. Its addressed to a C. Pillot. 

Find him, wherever he is, and deliver the note.

_nodes.key: quest:a skeleton eye for alyssa fearnon
_nodes.display_name: A Skeleton Eye for Alyssia Fearnon
_nodes.db_name: A Skeleton Eye for Alyssa Fearnon
_nodes.description:
Return a Skeleton Eye to Alyssia Fernon in Hidden Hills. 

She has warned you that they're hard to come by, but every now and then you'll find one on a skeleton of any sort. 

She suggested searching in the cave on the north side of Hidden Hills.

You've earned the favor of a mysterious brotherhood by finding their lost friend.

Per their instructions, you should make your way to the sandbar beneath the statue of Vitheo for a ride to the mainland.
Ghostly beings of Erenshor can leave behind Aetheria if you destroy them. If you find any, Saya Seawatcher in Vitheo's Watch says she has a unique reward for you.
Albixt Munger in Silkengrass fears that his missing wife is now one of the risen pilgrims in the area. If you find her engagement ring on one of them, return it to him for a reward.
_nodes.disabled_text: This is not it, I already have it.

_nodes.description:
Alice Hewer's necklace was eaten by a small crocodile near the lighthouse! 

If you find it, she would like for you to bring it back to her.
_nodes.disabled_text: You've already given me my necklace back. This one's not mine... weird that you found two!

_nodes.description:
Rumors of methods to access the Ethereal Planes have been persistent throughout history. 

There may be a way to reach places not meant for humans, and reap the rewards within.
Amethi, just outside of town in Stowaway's Step, has expressed interest in making and repairing keys... if you find any broken ones, bring them her way.
Liani Bosh in Port Azure has asked you to forge an Angler's Ring. With it, you can buy a trip back to Stowaway's Step.

Aragath claims to have been imprisoned by a group of necromancers. He thinks they're behind a hidden door in the Island Tomb near a Stone Throne. 

Destroy the necromancers and collect their soul-stones, and reunite those stones with their linked watchlights to absorb their power.

Return the four charged stones to Aragath.
_nodes.disabled_text: I will leave shortly, thank you.

_nodes.description:
A group of necromancers in the island tomb claim to be holding a demon named Aragath captive through magic. 

They have asked you to hand the demon an empty soulstone, to absorb his power and finally allow them to leave the island.
_nodes.disabled_text: I do not need anything else from you. Thank you for your help.

_nodes.description: A strange hermit in Azynthi's Garden has taken note of you shouting his name. He asked you to speak it to him in person.
A strange ghostly statue in the Jaws of Sivakaya claims to have 'awoken the guardian' and that you should let it look upon you for a moment.
PLACEHOLDER FOR CONTINUATION
Keep your eyes open for any intel on how or why Sivakayans are gaining access to Port Azure. Share it with Guard Captain Kilkay if you find it.
Fredrick in Port Azure would like bear meat.

A fisherman has brought you to a small island he happened upon. He claims that the Island matches the description of the lost Bellwain Isle, where a 100-year wanted Pirate retired into hiding with his wife with all of his treasures. 

The fisherman claims to have disturbed nothing, but something mysterious lingers in the air.
You've found some lost letters meant for Captain Bellwain, and you've given them back to Bellsie Bellwain.
You've handed the lost love letters to Bellwain, but he told you they were not his to take.
William Cardigo at Willowwatch has hinted that if you clear out the bonepits, and bring him proof of slaying the "glowing red demon" deep within, he'd make it worth your while.

Braxon Elden in the Braxonian Desert has expressed interest in the scent of Brashell emanating from the covered well that has been recently excavated. 

If you you happen across any Brashell in your explorations, Braxon Elden may be interested in seeing it.
_nodes.disabled_text: It's so interesting to see this!

_nodes.description: You showed the two pieces of the Braxonian Portal to Braxon Elden, and he was able to combine them.
You need to find an Eldrich Crystal to power the Rune of Brax, and offer both items to the energy source at his Portal.
You showed the writings from Fallen Braxonia to Braxon Elden, and he was most impressed.
_nodes.disabled_text: Ah, these are not new... just copies of what you've shown me.

_nodes.description: You handed a fisherman his lunch in in return he mentioned he may share a pirate tale.
You should ask the Shady Fisherman on the Port Azure docks to tell you a tale. He claims to have found something in the sea that may interest an adventurous person like you.

The Fisherman on the Azure Docks has promised you a trip to a lost Island in exchange for helping him finish his day's fishing quota. 

He needs 4 Octorunners.

Brackfruit Breaded Island Fin
1 Island Fin
1 Brackfruit Juice
1 Bread
1 Coal
You're not even sure if this really happened.
A Guard in Port Azure has offered you the job of Reliquary Caretaker. All you have to do is go inside and make sure everything is in order. After that, you can do whatever you'd like with it.
William Cardigo has asked you to find out what lurks in the Bonepits and clear it out before it emerges into Willowwatch.
_nodes.disabled_text: Thanks, but you gave me one of these already.

_nodes.description:
Lucian has asked for your help in completing the Stowaway Step Ritual. He needs the following legendary items:

A Loss Stone
A Remnant of Sivakaya
A brain warped  by the Sivakayan Dementia 
A Nightmare Crystal
The Rottenfoot Poachers have asked for Croc tails

Neziroth Daggerhart had his prized ancestral coin stolen by bandits somewhere in Stowaway's Step. 

If you find it, return it to him near the Shrine to Vitheo.
_nodes.disabled_text: What's this? A duplicate? This is definitely fake!

_nodes.description: You have demonstrated your faith in Sivakaya's Gift to proceed into the inner church of the Jaws of Sivakaya.
Edgar in Silkengrass has promised a generous reward for any Sivakayan Axes or Swords or Sceptres that you're able to bring him.
_nodes.disabled_text: I already have all I can eat!

_nodes.description: Varran Juctor in Fernalla Field would like some of the beads he sees the Risen Druids behind him wearing. Bring him as many as you can find.
You have been told that it may be possible to combine an Eldrich crystal with an inert rune at the right place of power in order to power the rune once again.

Nyltih in Hidden Hills has asked for Faerie Dust from the nearby forest.

It could be dropped by any Faerie that you find.
_nodes.disabled_text: Already got it from ya! Thanks anyhow.

_nodes.description: You've powered your Fernallan Rune

You've encountered a dying wish of Fernalla in the Blooming Sepulcher, and it has asked you to find her brother's tomb and defeat its guardian at the entry chamber. Upon doing so, you should return to this wish with a memory of the Burial

You've encountered a second wish of Fernalla in the Blooming Sepulcher. This one has asked you to find Balaste, in Vitheo's Rest, and see if you can sense a memory that might reveal if Vitheo was indeed laid to rest there, and what happened next.
A wish of Fernalla has asked that you find Elwio the Traitor in Vitheo's Rest, within the Traitor's Halls. She feels a great sadness for him, but cannot recall why.

Fernalla herself has asked that you find Vitheo and be sure his wordly remains are at rest. She has given you a key ingredient for lighting one of the braziers in his tomb to awaken the Guardian who watches over his inner sanctum's key.
Handing a Riftsnare Gem to the Flickering Souls in Azynthi's Garden pulls them into full existence...
You've found a blueprint for an Azynthian Resting Place and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for an Azynthian Dining Room and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for an Azynthian Livingroom and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for an Loomingwood Shrine and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for an Loomingwood Marketplace and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for an Loomingwood Shrine and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for a Stone Auction House and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for a Stone Bank and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for a Stone Dining Room and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for a Stone Fountain and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for a Stone Training Room and gave it to a furniture maker.
You've found a blueprint for a Stone Vendor and gave it to a furniture maker.
Benson Gamble in Hidden Hills is looking for Graveweed Leaf from the nearby Bonepits. It's located on the north side of Hidden Hills.

A Stormcaller named Windlae in Hidden Hills has asked that you find and fell a beast in the Brake called Grizzlepaw.

She has observed a remnant of a chain stuck in the beast's teeth that may have belonged to a friend of hers.
Hadden in Fernalla's Revival Plains has asked you to take a package of supplies to Ripper's Keep. They'll give you a receipt, and if you bring that back to Hadden he'll reward you.
Take the receipt of delivery from Ripper's Keep back to Hadden in Fernalla's Revival Plains.
You've heard rumors of an old tomb on the island that has attracted the attention of strange ritualists. It may be worth investigating if you can find it.
The statue of Sivakaya has gazed upon you.

Vethalo Gala in Port Azure's undercity tunnels has offered you a key to Vitheo's Rest - a hidden tomb beneath the city. 

He's asking for one Sivakrux as a trade.

_nodes.display_name: A Bounty on Guard Captain Kilkay
_nodes.description:
Ripper DeMerth wants you to kill Guard Captain Kilkay in Port Azure, for his role in the abandonment of the Citizens of the North.

Return with proof of his demise.
_nodes.disabled_text: This is not anything I'm interested in taking from you.

_nodes.description: Deliver the deportation papers to Kio the Light Keeper at the Lighthouse and collect his Gate Key for your own use.
_nodes.disabled_text: You've done this to me already! Why rub it in?

_nodes.description: You should find the recipient of this note, it looks as though it's never going to find its own way.
You have restored the Nighthollow Candle, but it is not lit. You must find a Braxonian Flame to ignite the wick and restore the Candle's true power.
Foreman Hauck in Port Azure has asked you to explore the Lost Cellar and find any Intel that may or may not exist down there. Bring it to him if you find it.
_nodes.disabled_text: Oh... they made copies of it? Good for the collection, I suppose.

_nodes.description: Handing an Essence of Lost Faith to a Stardust will take away its invulnerability.

You've found the writings of a treasure hunter and you found his phantom on the Duskenlight Coast near the Malaroth Nesting Grounds. He has asked you to continue the hunt in his name, and directed you to his wife for help. He does not recall where he lived, but he says it was in a busy place. He gave you a small pin to prove to his wife that you've met him.
_nodes.disabled_text: Oh my, someone made duplicates of these?

_nodes.description: You've reminded a phantom of who he was in a past life, and he has asked you to continue his treasure hunt. He gave you a pin and directed you to speak to his wife, using the pin as proof of having met him. He said she will help you pick up on his quest where he left off.
You have a deceased treasure hunter's journal, and a request from his widow to continue his quest. You should bring whatever you find back to him to help put him at rest.

You've made Malaroth Food with:
1 Octorunner
1 Frowney Clowney
1 Azure Mead
Prichard Zemoro has asked you to bring him a treasure map and an elixir of enlightment II. He says he'll give you a Sivakrux for each combo you bring. Prichard is in the slums of Port Azure.
There is a fisherman waiting for you under the cover of night in Blacksalt Strand to take you back to Stowaway's Step.

Xander Zaxtrough in Hidden Hills made mention of a missing person he's hunting for. He suspects that the Molorai Militia are holding this person somewhere on Erenshor. 

He mentioned Old Krakengard specifically.

Bring 5 Nagalok claws to Annaleigh in Salted Strand. 

She thinks she may be able to make something out of them that you would find useful.

Trevor Urchand in Hidden Hills is doing research on Eldoth Molorai. He suspects Eldoth has ties to the resurgence of Necromancy on Erenshor, but needs to know more. 

He says that there may be Necromancers in the Bonepits, and should you find any of their ritual books, he'd like you to bring it to him.
A note found on Raval's corpse was accepted by the Solunarian Holy Guard. He was not happy to read it.
Feed an Eye of Plaxitheris to Occuphage under the lighthouse.

A scribbled note from a madman in the desert has revealed that a mysterious stone that he was holding was the property of Orion Mycon in Port Azure. 

Orion may be interested in receiving it back...

Orion Mycon has given you his damaged ring. If you can fix it, it's yours. 

He said you should find a jeweler for more help.
Parmalee Gemlyn has asked you to bring back the broken ring, an Elderstone Shard, and a chunk of Citrine Sandsilver in order to repair Orion's Ring
_nodes.disabled_text: I can't do that again... it was too much for me.

_nodes.description: In return for Seaspice, Percy Ildia gave you a note with instructions to give it to the boat captain on the docks who was preparing to leave.

Percy has asked for some Sea Spice and in return he has promised passage off of the island. 

He is behind the registration office.
_nodes.disabled_text: Come on now... you're not that gullible!

_nodes.description: A portal exists between Blooming Sepulcher and Vitheo's Rest - you can re-activate it by bringing a piece of Vitheo's Rest to the Remnant in Blooming Sepulcher
You've achieved something impressive.

Raval, the First Paladin, has been trapped in an insanity wracked body for centuries. You've freed him and found his spirit, and his last wish is to know that Astra, the Celestial Dragon, has finally been removed from this plane.
_nodes.disabled_text: I'll leave shortly, thank you again.

_nodes.description:
Larviso in Loomingwood has told you about the Nighthollow Candle. It was stolen from The Wisp long ago. If you find any trace of it, bring it to him. 

He mentioned that a Sivakayan Darkwarden may have interest in it, or perhaps its lost in the Braxonian Desert... worse yet, an antiquities dealer may have recovered it.
_nodes.disabled_text: What is this, a replica? We have the real candle already.

_nodes.description:
Register yourself on the island by bringing your Dock Papers to Controller Wendyl in the registration office.

The registration office is the large building near the docks.

Go to the Gate and tell the Guard there that you've registered to gain a key to the pass through to the rest of Stowaway's Step. 

(To tell the guard you've registered, target him and press ENTER to type. Type 'I'm registered'.)
You've placed the Rune of the Hills in its receptacle in the Reliquary
You've placed the Rune of Ripper in its receptacle in the Reliquary
You've placed the Rune of Sands in its receptacle in the Reliquary
You've placed the Rune of Silkengrass in its receptacle in the Reliquary
You've placed the Rune of Soluna in its receptacle in the Reliquary
You've placed the Rune of Winds in its receptacle in the Reliquary

Guard Captain Kilkay in Port Azure was not pleased with the note Ripper had his men send along. 

He has warned you away from Ripper and his "traitorous army", and has said that if you can acquire their Insignia Badges, he'll reward you handsomly.
Ripper has mentioned rumors of Sivakayans in Port Azure. She has asked that you keep an eye open for evidence of that, and bring it to her should you find it.
Ripper has reviewed the encryption you brought to her and has determined that Sivakayans have gained access to Port Azure through the lighthouse. She has asked that you investigate.
_nodes.reward_item_key: item:quest - insignia of loyalty to azure

_nodes.description:
Guard Captain Kilkay in Port Azure has offered a reward for the death of Ripper DeMerth, a traitor to Port Azure.

Return with proof of the demise of Ripper.
_nodes.disabled_text: This is absurd. You've given me Ripper's ear already. Did you go back and cut off the other one?

_nodes.description: Mavik Estonia, of Ripper's Army, has requested you deliver a message to Guard Captain Kilkay in Port Azure.

Jeremie Galyard has offered to trade a trinket he found in Ripper's Keep that he says may have belonged to Azynthi. 

He wants 3 bottles of Azure Mead and a Seaspice.
_nodes.disabled_text: I'm still good pal, thanks anyhow.

_nodes.description:
Torado in Wardhaven has asked if you could help him gather components for The Rituals they must perform to restore Sivakaya. He needs:

1 Intact Faerie Wing
1 Devoratrix Thorn
1 Pink Pteriaped Feather
_nodes.disabled_text: I'm all set for now, but thanks

_nodes.description:
Alitho, a collector in Port Azure, has asked for you to bring him any Rottenfoot sashes that you may find. 

The Rottenfoot swamps would be a logical place to search for these sashes.
Samuel in Hidden Hills has asked you to deliver a note to his good friend Harvey Alder, who is likely staying at the Azure Arms in Port Azure.
_nodes.disabled_text: Are you making forgeries now? Get a life.

_nodes.description: You've found ancient Siva-Braxonian teachings that reveal some surprising world history. You've given them to Braxon Michaen who was very impressed.

Serra Stile lost her family heirloom shovel in the Elderstone Mines incident. 

She believes it was taken by a slime. If you happen across it, return it to her.
_nodes.disabled_text: This is someone else's I think. I have mine already.

_nodes.description:
You've met a Shard of Sivakaya inside the portal at the Stowaway's Step Ritual. She warned you of a corruption taking over Erenshor, and asked that you help free the Gods who have touched this world. 

She faded out of existence after her warning, cautioning you of a manifesting corruption nearby.
You met a strange being called "The Curator" on Shivering Step
You've made quite a mark on Shivering Step. It's time to go home.
_nodes.disabled_text: Ah, deja vu is a fancy thing.

_nodes.description:
You've been pulled into a game of hide and seek... 

Someone (or something) is waiting to be found on Shivering Step.
You met a strange being called "The Gatekeeper" on Shivering Step
You've learned that seeing is not always easy, but is possible in any light.
You met a strange being called "The Harbinger" on Shivering Step
It may be beneficial to find a way to change the lighthouse color to brighten up the island further...
_nodes.disabled_text: ... *The flame flares, but does not change.*

_nodes.description: Kio has summoned you to the lighthouse to get your lamp for whatever he has in store next.
Find the four spirits of Shivering Step.
You should find a way to ignite the Brazier deep in the cold Island Cave.
You met a strange being called "The Bridgekeeper" on Shivering Step

_nodes.key: quest:a magical sword in port azure
_nodes.display_name: A Magical Sword in Port Azure
_nodes.db_name: A Magical Sword in Port Azure
_nodes.description:
There is a magical sword to be had in Port Azure. 

Find a Ruby and two Seaspices for Tristian, who is loitering in the back lots of the houses near the main gate.

_nodes.key: quest:a sivakayan horn for ludo luxo
_nodes.display_name: A Sivakayan Horn for Ludo Luxo
_nodes.db_name: A Sivakayan Horn for Ludo Luxo
_nodes.description:
Ludo Luxo in Hidden Hills has asked for you to find a Sivakayan Horn from the Bonepits - a cave on the north side of Hidden Hills. 

They make great sharpening stones.

A recovered page of Sivakayan Intel was handed to Guard Captain Kilkay, who was appreciative of the info.

He has asked you to continue to search for information on how Sivakayans may be entering Port Azure.
A recovered page of Sivakayan Intel was handed to Guard Captain Kilkay, who was appreciative of the info.
A recovered journal of a Sivakayan Darkwardenl was handed to Guard Captain Kilkay, who was appreciative of the info.
Ripper asked for you to bring any information you find on Sivakayan activity to her.
Supply Sergeant Tipp wants Sivakayan Ore. He says their soldiers carry it sometimes, but it's hard to find.
_nodes.disabled_text: I have all I need, thank you for your help.

_nodes.description:
Despite reports of Sivakayan raiders headed to Port Azure, none have been seen within the city. 

It is suspected that they may be lurking inside the city, somewhere in the shadows. 

If you're able to locate them, report to Captain Kilkay.
_nodes.disabled_text: I've seen this already. If you find more, bring it to me.

_nodes.description:
The Guard Captain in Port Azure has reviewed the Sivakayan Encryption that you have found and has determined that the Sivakayans have created a secret access point in the Port Azure Lighthouse.

He has given you a key and asked you to investigate.
_nodes.reward_item_key: item:quest - insignia of loyalty to azure
_nodes.disabled_text: I've seen this already. If you find more, bring it to me.

_nodes.description:
Once wielded by Soluna herself, the blade was forged from the metal of a moon and bathed in the flames of stars. It is rumored to exist on this plane. 

If your travels bring you across clues, take note.
You've found a Paladin trapped by Azynthi who claims to have had knowledge of Soluna's Celestial Blade stolen from him. If you can restore him, he may be able to share it with you.
TO BE CONTINUED
You've powered your Solunarian Rune
Capture Leah's soul from her watchlight on Stowaway's Step
Capture Posta's soul from his watchlight on Stowaway's Step
Capture Revid's soul from his watchlight on Stowaway's Step
Capture Stalzo's soul from his watchlight on Stowaway's Step
You have found a way to move the fallen angel statue out of the way on Shivering Step
Through Larviso, Sivayaka has shown you that Azynthi's corruption has taken hold of all of the Gods of Erenshor. Fernalla, Vitheo, Soluna, and Brax.
You are stranded on Stowaway's Step. There has to be a way off... You should ask around and see what you can learn.
Ta'Dah, the hidden Sorcerer for Ripper has stopped you and offered you a prize for collecting three intact mushroom caps from the cave near Ripper's Keep.
_nodes.disabled_text: I do not need more, but thank you.

_nodes.description: You've found a strange boatman in The Blight and presented your found "Boat Pass" to him. He will grant you passage to Azynthi's Garden now, at your leisure.

To ignite, combine the following in the Dark Flame Brazier in Vitheo's Rest:
2 Blight Crystals
1 Blightwood slab
1 Spark of the First Flame

The Ceremonial Flame requires:
1 Ancient Coal
1 Elderstone Shard
1 Wolf Meat
1 Pristine Grass Spider Carapace

To ignite, combine the following in the Life Flame Brazier in Vitheo's Rest:
1 Essence of Nature
1 Gift of Fernalla
1 Loomingwood Branch
1 Droplet of the First Frost

A Ghostly Figure has handed you a stone that contains the very sadness that has been brought upon the world by Azynthi. She instructed you to bring it to someone who might know its true power, she suggested someone who mourns the loss of Sivakaya may be best.
_nodes.disabled_text: It's so incredible! Get it to Lucian.

_nodes.description: You've been told that it's imperative to get the Loss Stone back to Lucian at Stowaway's Step.

You've found a note in the pocket of Brice Mathers. It instructs you to find his brother in Goodsoil. 

His brother's name is Yarren - if you cannot find him, ask for him.
_nodes.disabled_text: You have the key... if you've lost it I don't know what to tell you. I do not have another.

_nodes.description:
Yarren Mathers has given you a key to an old church he says is buried in the hills of Blacksalt Strand. 

Locate the church, and see what secrets it holds. 

Return any tomes you find to Yarren and he may share more of what he knows with you.
_nodes.disabled_text: We've covered this already... show me anything new you find!

_nodes.description:
Yarren Mathers has studied the Chapters of Descent you found in the old Church.

The tale of Sivakaya, Azynthi, and The Wisp has become more clear, but is still shrouded in mystery. 

Yarren has asked you to search for more pages from the Chapters of Descent.
_nodes.disabled_text: We have seen these pages already, have you forgotten?

_nodes.description:
The Ceremonial Flame in Fernalla's Revival Plains requires:

1 Ancient Coal
1 Faerie Dust
1 Remnant of a Day Dream
1 Remnant of a Nightmare
The odd Hermit has given you a Riftstone and instructed you to find "The Riftsnares" to "Pull the four souls into this realm and destroy them."

The Ceremonial Flame at Ripper's Keep requires:

1 Sivakrux
1 Planar Stone Shard
1 Ghostly Giant's Blood
1 Unborn Phobia

The village of Goodsoil in Fernalla's Revival Plane was destroyed by forces unknown. 

Rumors are abound, but figuring out what truly happened may take more time.
Sivakaya has asked you to obtain the Secrets of Creation from the corruption that will manifest in the Stowaway's Step Portal. She wants you to bring it to The Wisp.
_nodes.disabled_text: You must find a way to reach the Gods!

_nodes.description: You tried to give the Secrets of Creation to Lucian on Stowaway's Step, but he instructed you to bring it to Larviso in Loomingwood instead.
If you find four pieces of a treasure map, you can bring it to Cecil in Faerie's Brake to have it repaired.
You've returned a compass to Asaga Underloft in Port Azure, and he has asked you to continue to explore the mysterious portals in search of his missing father.
_nodes.disabled_text: What is this? Are you making these yourself? Is this some sort of a trick?

_nodes.description: Valrae Cleary in Faerie's Brake has asked you to find her a set of keys to Krakengard. The Bunk key and the Tower key are what she's after.
_nodes.disabled_text: I have these... didn't you give them to me?

_nodes.description: Valrae Cleary in Faerie's Brake has asked you to bring her four Ogrespice leaves. Who knows what for?
_nodes.disabled_text: I have these... didn't you give them to me?

_nodes.description: You've powered your Vithean Rune

Wally Waldorf is near the shop in Stowaway's Step and he really wants a Moongill fish. 

Moongill are known to come out at night and are famously hard to catch.
_nodes.disabled_text: I already have all I can eat!

_nodes.description: Jazmyn Challa in Port Azure has asked for you to bring back any clues that might prove her claim that Reavers are the true first people of Erenshor.
_nodes.disabled_text: Another copy... incredible. Keep that for yourself and enjoy the tale within.

_nodes.description:
The odd cultists won't say much, but one has mistaken you for their friend 'Wyland' before realizing his error.

If you find this 'Wyland', he may be holding a note or a clue as to what they're up to...

You have gathered seeds from all of the known Willows in Erenshor and given them to a Watcher high upon a hill in Willowwatch. 

In return, the Watcher has given you a special lens and claimed it will allow its wearer to spot hidden Sivakayans throughout the world. They are thought to wander nearly everywhere on occassion.
_nodes.disabled_text: I need but one seed from each tree, no more are necessary.

_nodes.description: Cagnus Marlsen in Willowwatch has asked for you to clear out the old Willow shrine so they can use the fertile soil to grow crops for their settlement.
_nodes.disabled_text: Yeah ok I don't need all of it in bags, knock it off!

_nodes.description: You have returned a journal to Wishlan that he lost ages ago. He was gracious, and offered you an old trinket he once held dear.
Wishlan handed you a bag of stones and asked for one to keep.
Fredrick in Port Azure would like wolf meat.

You've found a note on the Ghost of Wyland Sercher. 

Upon reading it, it has instructed you to speak a secret password to an "Elder Brother at the Ritual". 

Approach his brothers, and speak the word to them.
Cloth Armwraps
They fit poorly.
Armguards commonly worn by the Knights of Soluna
You can check out your muscles and your reflection at the same time.
This used to be normal plate armor, but now it's covered in a strange fungus.
Yarrrrr! You're a pirate!
Light and airy.
Made of some sort of insect shell
Simply unbreakable
They're etched in faded Braxonian writings
It bears the insignia of the Islander Bandits.
A strange mix of physical and mental energy lingers
Torn by the teeth of an imaginary beast

Our resplendent Goddess of War was not always veiled in shadow, nor did her heart once beat with the wicked cadence of necromantic sorcery. It is an unspoken truth, a silence that echoes through our ancient hallways.

The wisp's malevolent touch, like a ghostly hand upon the pages of our Codex, has cast aspersions upon the very essence of our goddess, obscuring the truths that lay concealed in the labyrinthine corridors of her past and then rewrote her motives...

...and then the wisp, after that tumultuous hour, severed ties with the goddess, their bond sundered by a schism that hinted at a greater, impending evil lurking in the void, an evil that drove Sivakaya to make her tragic choice to safeguard this world from an unfathomable calamity. 

The wisp's enigmatic departure stands as a testament to the complexities of divine sacrifice, and the shrouded lore that surrounds the goddess's descent from grace as she woefully embraced her new twisted and broken form.

The Wisp dares to defy the divine metamorphosis bestowed upon Sivakaya by Azynthi. Their misguided pursuits to strip her of the bestowed power and revert to a perceived former glory are treacherous. 

Sivakaya's transformation is no descent from grace but an ascension to a realm of dark magnificence, and all who fail to stand against those who seek to unravel this sublime tapestry woven by the shadows themselves shall fall to it.

Brax, once the beacon of power, now obscured in the fog of negligence. Fernalla, the tender nurturer, now tainted by indifference to the cries of her faithful. Vitheo, the arbiter of justice, has turned a blind eye to the righteous pleas of the downtrodden. Soluna, once the luminous guide, is veiled in apathy, forsaking the path of enlightenment. 
We cast our judgment upon these wayward gods, for their negligence and abandonment. For none have sacrificed as Sivakaya has.

In the wake of her transformation, the faithful of Sivakaya found themselves torn. Some held fast to the vision of her selfless sacrifice, a testament to her resolve to shield the world from ruin. 

Others were captivated by the dark power bestowed by Azynthi, heeding the call to arms that echoed in her name. Yet, beneath both of their fervors, an unseen dementia took root, shared with a subtle fracture in devotion as shadows twisted their hearts and blurred the lines of loyalty.

And so they parted ways—some drawn to the clamor of distant battles, others to the silence of candlelit halls, each seeking to honor her as they saw fit. 

Though no open rift split them, a quiet discord lingered, and in time, their paths diverged into unfamiliar silence—two fragments of a once-unified devotion, now strangers to one another but always united in the watch for Eldrin's deceitful hand.

She was equally lost to all, who were merely orphaned children, vying for her blessings. In misguided destruction or quiet meditation - her children wait.

As the faithful toiled in dark rooms by candlelight, even her steed decayed within the shadowed depths beneath sacred halls, holding vigil in the hollow stillness and the growing absence of hope.

And the Wisp's plots weave through the land, working to undo the beauty that has befallen Sivakaya

Yet there lingers a quiet hope, whispered among the faithful like the soft rustling of leaves in hidden groves. They speak of a return—a day when Sivakaya, untouched by the Wisp's grasp, will rise anew, her true form radiant with dark splendor and reunite her children.

Sireaethe has emerged from the shadows, claiming Sivakaya's presence within herself, and sharing her words with the faithful. The course is true, and all must remain steadfast in their resolve - for from beyond, she observes with approval.

Behold, my loyal disciples, the whispers of the past have revealed a hidden tapestry woven beneath the desert's shifting veil! History herself beckons us to unearth the buried legacy of a forgotten civilization, a trove of secrets that shall eclipse the feeble ambitions of Port Azure and its covetous marauders. 

It is our sacred duty to defy these interlopers, to stand as guardians of the ancient mysteries that pulse with the divine essence coursing through this forsaken realm.

It is imperative that we remain in close proximity to the unfolding discoveries. Let the echoes of untainted history be our compass, and only when it becomes apparent that the Braxons are on the verge of unlocking the buried truths, shall you, my brethren, step forth. Interfere with the subtlety of shadows, for we must ensure that the power concealed within this ancient civilization is harnessed for our ascendancy alone.

The Wisp weaves its shadowy tendrils through the tapestry of this land, and only you, my trusted followers, are privy to its true purpose while the world rests peacefully on its bed of ignorance. 

Guard the secrets we unearth with utmost vigilance, for they hold the key to thwarting The Wisp's designs, lest we gain another obstacle to our endeavor.

The mystery of the sealed door to the forgotten catacomb looms like a specter. Here, a spirit's silent whispers weave tales of a lost candle, its value long obscured by the passage of time. 

Oh, how we mourn the absence of clarity, as we contemplate whether this elusive flame holds the key to unlocking the secrets hidden within the depths of the earth.

Blessed be we, chosen custodians of Erenshor, and as such must strive to ensure its wonder remains untainted by the grasping hands of politics and petty squabbles of men influenced by modernity

As such custodians, constant in our determination to prevail and bathed in promised wonder, we march forth.

Erenshor holds within its soul the very artifacts and treasures once used to construct our realm, steeped in turmoil of bygone eras, stained by the very essence of time itself, and so susceptible to the ruin of politics and the common man's hunger for power.

My friends, it is our burden to repel these threats.

So then arise, my companions, my friends, my soldiers. We, an indomitable legion of purity, can and might deliver prosperity to this plane.  

Let not the whispers of our intentions reach the ears of the uninitiated, for the power we seek is not for the unworthy.

Discretion is our armor, and fearlessness our blade.

We, the architects of destiny - the shepherds of our own legacy, cloaked in our armor and clutching our blades, driven by our determination to experience wonder - will bask in the light of a future only we may build. 

In blood shall we forge our legacy that transcends time - written in a language of ancient power and punctuated by the extraordinary sacrifices you may be asked to make.

We stand not just as seekers of mere trinkets, but as chosen custodians of the divine treasures hidden within this sacred land. The spirits of antiquity have anointed us with this privilege. 

Port Azure and its greedy marauders seek to plunder what is rightfully ours! It is your sacred duty to defy these interlopers. The land echoes with the cries of its untainted history, urging us to stand resolute.  Rise, my brethren, and let the divine essence that courses through this place fuel the flames of our conviction!

There stands upon this isle, a strange hermit - a recluse steeped in secrets, harboring knowledge that dances on the edge of forbidden. Azynthi's name is whispered in the same breath as his.

To confront him is to unravel the web that binds them both, yet caution whispers its own warnings, for there lies an untangible chill within him.

There is a reason why his name is uttered only in whispers. One should dare not do more.

Our necromancies diverge: mine trades blood for undying light; yours would restore light by bathing it in torment. Vitheo lies beyond my remedy, but you will pursue your own. 

I have given you the place and the way. In return, you will bring pressure to bear on Port Azure.  

May Sivakaya find the spark she seeks within Vitheo's husk - a nearer conduit to his ascended self, and thus to our reclamation.

-Eldoth
A Rune that may one day get you to Brax's Elemental Plane. It needs to be paired with a power source at a place where Brax still has a presence.
A Rune that may one day get you to Fernalla's Plane of the Willow. It needs to be paired with a power source at a place where Fernalla still has a presence.
A Rune that may one day get you to Soluna's Celestial Plane. It needs to be paired with a power source at a place where Soluna still has a presence.
A Rune that may one day get you to Vitheo's Plane of Honor. It needs to be paired with a power source at a place where Vitheo still has a presence.

A cave sits dark, hollow and cold,
Its flame long dead, its soul grew old

With Eternal Ice and Death's Dust true,
An Aged Tooth and a Thief's touch too,
The soul of a beast makes the flames renew

The flame will rise with a deathly heat,
As the living decay, their quest complete.
Squishy.
Smooth Ivory from a large beast
Gamey.

Proof that supplies were delivered to Ripper's Keep.
An enchanted branch
Born from an ignored wish... a tangible sadness can be felt from this wispy entity. Someone has given up all hope. This energy could weaken even the most optimistic forces.

Never before have I witnessed a curse so relentless! Ancient and unyielding, it condemns us to a cold, half-living state, our flesh turning to stone, and our minds slowly succumbing to madness. In fleeting moments of clarity, I try to write, but I sense those moments are growing ever shorter, fading into a darkness I can barely resist.

The fits of rage seem to ease when we are alone together, so we will seek refuge far from civilization, hoping to find a way to reverse the cold hardening of our bodies.


What could have caused this fate to befall us? Has Soluna awakened some unseen evil in the air?

The rage rises once more. Seclusion cannot keep it at bay forever, and death eludes us. I fear our downfall is inevitable.


Too often, I long to inflict the pain that consumes me.

Each day, I no longer pray for clarity or salvation from the rage.


Instead, I pray for an unsuspecting traveler to cross my path.


Tearing them apart brings a twisted joy.


Only then can I write. Only then do I feel relief.
A somewhat rare stone, said to hold magical power in the right hands.
It squirms about
A mixture of ground meat and bones.
A mixture of ground meat and bones. Something seems off about it.
Hard scales from the hide of a Malaroth Beast.

The invader that looms threatens all we hold dear. Stand vigilant, for the hour of reckoning approaches, and our allegiance shall be tested.

Hold fast in the Swamp of Rottenfoot. Do not advance to Port Azure without further word. Our Lady Sivakaya, wrapped in her new cloak of dark, demands our loyalty. Await the courier for guidance.

In Faith of Azynthi,

-Darkwarden Ealdred

It recalls images of Vitheo's mortal form being laid to rest, surrounded by his most loyal followers. A notable gap between two men can be seen - someone is missing.

The missing person is not mourned, but hated. He has done something terrible. 

Balaste lays a hand on Vitheo's helmet, and the memory fades.

It recalls sorrow. A task has been given by a dying hero that cannot be declined. Her voice echoes as her spirit ascends, visibly battling a darkness with her own light. 

"Spare him of this", it cries. "If the odds say he will fall by his mother's twisted hand, fell him by your own - for she will never let go, not even in death."

"You must not share this wish - it must be you who carries the burden. Your mortal legacy will be tarnished, but your immortal legacy will be revered by the Gods..." and the voice fades away.

It recalls a glorious task - be granted a place of rest at the entrance of Vitheo's tomb, forever to guard its doors by presence alone. 

Memories of Vitheo's burial swirl, not clear, but still definite. 

Echoes of the Fernalla Plains Willow invade your mind, as though the memory originates from within the tree's very roots.

It recalls many emotions: Confusion, sorrow, and gratitude. Images of battle and betrayal swirl within. Images of his sister's face and voice, greeting him from a new and immortal place as he unwillingly leaves his mortal form. 

She is twisted and wrong, gripped by something that is familiar but vague. "I am so sorry," she whispers. 

A feeling of comfort, and the memory fades.
No matter how far you drift, one toss and you're pulled back to your anchor.
It's so sharp that you really can't not cut yourself on it when you handle it. Ow!
Inscribed faintly in the handle: "Eldrin"
It burns bright in your periphery, but when you look upon it, it turns to an impossibly black color.

A strange clump of dried  candle wax, breaking it open reveals a strange glow that quickly fades away. Visions sparkle across your eyes of a great Braxonian flame and hidden visages sharing truths. A strange word comes to mind... "Eldrin".

An old candle wick. Its tendrils flow in the breeze, and it whispers visions of shadows and tragedy into your mind as you hold it. As you study it you involuntarily mouth the word "Eldrin..."

Who is Eldrin?
Madness flows within where unending night terrors are held captive, safe from light.

To L. Kilfa

I hope this note finds you well. Our journey is set to begin tomorrow, and with any luck we'll arrive to see you within the week. 

Our training can begin at once. 

Until then, 
Bassalt Vallano

"My Dear Friend C. Pillot,

My concerns grow. Sivakayans have made the first move as we've all feared they would. If Fernalla remains able, she may be weighing options. This land cannot sustain a battle between Gods - we must seek the advice of The Wisp. We must make them see us. 

You have the key to their attention. It's time to bring it to existence."
A strange stone covered in what appears to be images of Brax and his Lover. It contains a small, coin shaped slot.

The faithful kneel before the flame, laying a stone paired with a token of his Lover, its surface warm with ancient memory. Beside it, a tool or token of their choosing—a silent request for favor.

One paired stone speaks a whisper. Two, a plea. Three, a chorus the Elder God cannot ignore. A request for favor is essential, but granting may not be.
Makes an absolutely awful tea, but people buy it.

It should be unaccessible to any who seek to enter. 

If the materials are not here on the island, use whatever you can until the proper materials arrive.

It's faded but legible:

Find a reliable transit through the pass for our needs. We've established a foothold in the North away from the Molorai and other prying eyes. 

"Common thieves" is a term that will no longer apply to us if we can achieve our goal.

Continue to capture and study them. Allow the tamers to feed them to attract their strongest, and observe their ways and weaknesses.

I am counting on you!

The North Villages are in ruin, their citizens have been slaughtered, only to be raised again by the Sivakayan demons to serve against our kind.

Your decision to withdraw your soldiers will be the most destructive in Erenshor's history for centuries to come.

We'll remain here to prevent the Sivakayans from decending on your city, however, know that once that threat is quelled, we plan to deal with you ourselves. 

Regards, Mavik Estonia, on behalf of Ripper

_nodes.description:
Finally, we have discovered the door to the ancients, but Evadne holds the only key. She will not be felled without first quelling the remnants of Brax's flames from which she feeds.

The stone I have procured from Orion Mycon for that purpose has bled away its power, as Orion was able to retain its band during our struggle.

Perhaps with Elderstone, we can forge another. Then we might be reunited with our lost ancestors once and for all.

The forays into The Blighted Desert have been fruitful, and we must continue. 

Locating our lost artifacts is an important step to unearthing fallen Braxonia. 

We will uncover the portal to the elemental planes and reclaim whats ours.

It trembles as you hold it and its aroma smells like fear and nightmares. It clips handily onto your belt though, and just having it in your inventory will do well enough. 

Passive: Place it in a <color=green> cosmetic </color> general inventory slot to enable its environmental light.

Together, the lovers created the land, and opened its gates to those who would come to settle - people of all faiths would come to be, and in peace they would live beneath the embrace of Siva and Brax - they would want for nothing and need for nothing, so it was decided.

To protect their paradise, Siva created a warrior unmatched by any who came before, and so not to encroach on the kingdom of Brax, she imbued him with the power of water and the speed of air. He was named Vitheo.

His oath to Sivakaya was sworn on the very altar upon which he was built, undying in its truth and unwavering in its tethers, it was forever done.

His contribution was not to security, but to sustenance and life. Brax created Fernalla, a demi-goddess in the image of Siva, she was born from life itself, and her fertility and pleasure spread throughout Erenshor causing the land to prosper with vibrance and joy.

Perfection had been achieved at the hand of the lovers, and together, they would be evermore.
An ancient currency, lost to time.
The writing is impossible to decipher, but bits of the drawing look a lot like Port Azure.

We humbly seek your continued support in unearthing the arcane secrets of Azynthi and channeling the stolen power into your formidable being, to illuminate the path lost to our people.

We vow to steadfastly defend against Port Azure, ensuring your endeavors remain unimpeded.

As you ascend to divinity, may you reign as a powerful God.

A sect of non-believers has defected from Port Azure and invaded the North Keep.  Their prowess and affinity for battle is a concern, if their efficiency is not unique among their masses, calling upon Azynthi should be a consideration.

Port Azure seems similarly well defended. Their internal strife seems not to have weakened their defenses. 

We will continue otherwise as planned with the infiltrations.

Its ancient scrawling words are mostly illegible except for two sentences near the bottom:

"Only he who dons a pin shall be welcomed, blessed by Brax or by Sivakaya - it matters not, any Braxonian or Sivakayan historian might gift status to a pupil."

A boatman will be at the blight docks indefinitely, should any Sivakayan officers require passage. 

Present these documents.

It would be ill advised to travel to The Garden without purpose. There is no time for idle audience.

E.M.

Only the Brazier within the coastal temple can ignite the portal.

With ancient coal for ignition, the most perfect carapace of a grass spider for a bowl to hold the meat of a wolf to summon his spirit, and a shard of Elderstone to enhance the flame, the gate may awaken.

I suspect I've uncovered the ingredients for the Fernallan Portal's ritual! I must obtain the remnants of a Daydream and a Nightmare from the Dusken Portal - a task that should prove simple with caution and blade. 

With those, it's simply a matter of procuring a dash of Faerie Dust and Aged Coal to ignite the flame.

We will stay the course and begin exports back to Merosavilla where armies will pay fortunes for such creatures. 

It is vital though, that in mixing the food we avoid adding the augmentation to the formula. It only invites disaster. Equal parts Octorunners, Clowny Fish, and some Azure Mead does the trick for attracting them. Use the Garden Shed oven.

I fear for our safety. I fear that our time is nearing its end. 

There have been cloaked figures lurking on the beach at night, after dark. I wonder if they know what knowledge I possess... and how they plan to extract it from me. I can only wonder what Eldoth has asked of them...

If you find this letter after my demise, see my Brother Yarren in Goodsoil. I've given something of great interest to him for safe keeping. Ask for him - he's often toiling away out of sight.

Nazura and Elwio and their abomination are sealed away in the Traitor's halls. May history forever look upon them in dismay. 

The resting Abomination clutches the key to Vitheo's resting place. I could not free it from his grasp, and I am confident no others will find success in that endeavor either.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

The Bonepits... a place of great misery, not mystery. The rumors of riches here were quickly replaced by rusted metals and decaying gemstones, all seasoned by a great dose of undead stink.

I can cross this hole off my list and proceed on. The old ruins on the Vithean Watch coast hold some promise - I will go there next.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

Krakengard proved interesting - an old Sivakayan fortress perhaps, its designs seemed to favor candlelight. 

It proved hopeless on clues though, and I shall continue my quest elsewhere. The Molorai troops are moving in, clearing the spiders and rats, and I am allowing them to evict me as well, as to avoid conflict with them. 

I will perhaps try my luck in Underspine Hollow.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

When you think you've met the most dense being, it will be an ogre. Never before have I seen such brute-driven stupidity. 

A simple bit of concealing foliage, and I was free to roam. I unearthed nothing except maddening babbling and ill-located latrines. 

I've heard a rumor of an excavation under Port Azure... I will investigate there next.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

Wow, just wow! It's amazing that there are hints of truth to the old stories about this place! The caverns gave way to old architecture and lost parts of the castle itself! 

Despite the spectral guards and overgrown insects, I managed to make my way through, and disappointingly, found nothing. 

I've caught wind of an event at the Elderstone Mines, perhaps my answers lie there.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

The miners have cracked into something magnificent and terrifying, and slinking through the caverns was no easy feat. 

While many of the lizard men and the slimes carried noticable artifacts, none had that shared designs with the one which I seek. It is safe to eliminate this place from consideration.

I suppose I will head to Rockshade Hold and see if the rumors are true about that place.

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

It's tragic what descended upon the men of Rockshade Hold. They are doomed to walk unaging and cursed and - unfortunately - without even a hint of that which I seek. The stonemen can keep their hold, I am moving on to Abyssal Lake! 

I've heard rumors of a secret entrance!

A Treasure Hunter's Journal Entry

Alas, I fear I have met my demise. Abyssal Lake was filled not with dumb beasts but stalking Sivakayans! 

They have sat me at a table with a chalice of wine and a hardy meal, and have interrogated me to no end for the secret of that which I seek. They will dispatch me from this life, I fear. I only hope to rest along the Western Sea Coast, for its sunsets are unmatched.
A pin worn by the phantom of a failed treasure hunter. It is inscribed: "To Benjamin, my true treasure. From Julie"

A Treasure Map. 

It indicates that this treasure is guarded by horrifying beings. Proceed with great caution.

Right Click to Reveal Treasure Zone. Only one map can be active at a time. Reading a new map will overwrite the first. 

(Failing the event will lose the treasure)

Splash a measure of ghostly blood

Wishlan is our last hope, but kneeling at the feet of a corruptor of Sivakaya might incur the wrath of Brax himself, which, while final, would still be merciful.

Bellsie loved her detective novels. Loved to talk about how she could be an investigator and how good she'd be at it. Things like "hide in plain sight" and "they never look up, so hide things up high!"

She even went on a tangent about how the roof would be the best place to hide your most valuable items. Nobody ever robs a roof.
It's a pile of never-sent letters, seemingly written to Bellwain by Bellsie while he was away at sea. It feels wrong to read them.

Bellsie alwas said that the bathtub is in a dreadfully drafty spot. She said the walls held nothing back, and implored me to move the source to another room, however, it was architecturally impossible to do that.

Warming up by the fire after a bath is not the worst, after all.

Bellsie always preferred to relax on the back patio, though she worried greatly about the eroding retaining wall that held it all together. 

I had promised to have a swim around it to verify its integrity, but it wasn't until I was making preparations to leave that I noted the extent of its disrepair. 

Bellsie always was right.

In decidedly feminine handwriting:

The Day Ends
5pm brings the orange glow of the cooking fire
6pm, the sky burns gently with that same warmth and desire


Evenings spent laughing and love - forever and always together.

This is not a place to be alone. It is with age and sorrow that I leave tomorrow for one final voyage to the sea. I will not return to this place, for nothing holds me here. I've given back the materials, and now I give myself back to this world. 

I can only hope to be reunited with Bellsie one day, but alas, I do not deserve such a kind fate. 

Returning home can't always be the same gift that it was the day before.

Bellsie always carried her flint, now it's been confined to my safe - for losing it would be be removing the last of her from this place.

If only I could recall the beautiful array of colors she would create at the Angel Torches, it would be like being with her one last time... I can feel a strong presence of her in that place and I long for it back.

"The Garden Song"
By Bellsie Bellwain

Bellsie always came here to "wash off the smoke" after our drinks at the pergola campfire. 

Thing is, she always would forget her drink and made me come back to the bar to get it for her. I'd leave it on her chair if she was off in the gardens.

Truly a nuisance in the moment, but a task I miss dearly now.

Our favorite wine was...  illicit. It's hard to source, and so I've resorted to keeping a stash in a nook beneath the docks. It's not the most accessible but its safe from prying eyes. 

I could not fathom running out, Bellsie would be heartbroken too. 

I had never planned to sail again, unless it was for our wine.
A key to the Bellwain Estate's Study

Bellsie always said the Sun Lilies bloomed at 6pm, but I've never seen them in the garden, even though she sang her song there each day for the plants.

I will not forget her voice, I wish I could remember the words.

Should any set  foot in this home, I implore you to return from where you came. It holds loss and sorrow and hopelessness - all of the wealth i have sailed for is worthless alone. 

My love, my light, my Bellsie is no more, and so shall I return to the sea one last time. 

I have narrated our lives here if you are inclined to stay, please honor our memories. 

-Bellwain
